key_points: An algebraic expression is formed using variables, constants and mathematical operations
Terms in an algebraic expression are separated by addition or subtraction signs
Like terms have the same variables with the same powers
Unlike terms have different variables or different powers
The distributive property helps in expanding algebraic expressions
Multiplication of algebraic expressions follows distributive law
Simplification of expressions requires combining like terms
Algebraic identities help in simplifying multiplication of expressions
formulas: (a + b)(c + d) = ac + ad + bc + bd
a(x + y + z) = ax + ay + az
exam_tips: Always apply distributive property carefully when expanding brackets
Combine only like terms while simplifying expressions
Check the sign of each term during multiplication
Write each step clearly to avoid algebra mistakes
Practice expansion and simplification problems regularly
